name: schemas
context: Define Zod schemas for any data shape — primitives, objects, arrays, enums, unions, tuples, records, and recursive types
info: This skill covers creating Zod schemas for every data shape an agent encounters. Primitives: z.string(), z.number(), z.boolean(), z.date(), z.bigint(), z.symbol(), z.undefined(), z.null(), z.void(), z.any(), z.unknown(), z.never(), z.nan(). Collections: z.object() for structured data, z.array() for lists, z.tuple() for fixed-length typed arrays, z.record() for string-keyed maps, z.map() and z.set() for ES6 collections. Enums: z.enum() for string enums, z.nativeEnum() for TypeScript enums. Composition: z.union() for OR types, z.discriminatedUnion() for tagged unions, z.intersection() for AND types. Special: z.literal() for exact values, z.lazy() for recursive schemas, z.promise() for Promise validation, z.function() for function signatures. Object schemas support .extend(), .merge(), .pick(), .omit(), .partial(), .required(), .passthrough(), .strict(), .strip(), and .catchall(). See [[zod/validation]] for constraints and [[zod/transform]] for data transformation.
triggers: create a union type with zod
define zod schema for API response

sections.title: Primitive schemas
sections.body: Zod provides schema constructors for every JavaScript primitive: `z.string()`, `z.number()`, `z.boolean()`, `z.date()`, `z.bigint()`, `z.symbol()`, `z.undefined()`, `z.null()`, `z.void()`, `z.any()`, `z.unknown()`, `z.never()`, `z.nan()`. Each returns an immutable schema instance with `.parse()`, `.safeParse()`, and all standard schema methods. Use `z.unknown()` over `z.any()` when you want type safety — `z.any()` disables type checking entirely.

sections.title: Object schemas and manipulation
sections.body: Create object schemas with `z.object({ key: schema })`. Access `.shape` for the schema map, `.keyof()` for a ZodEnum of keys. Manipulate with `.extend()` to add fields, `.merge()` to combine schemas, `.pick()` / `.omit()` to select fields, `.partial()` / `.required()` to toggle optionality. Control unknown key handling: `.strip()` (default — removes unknown), `.passthrough()` (keeps unknown), `.strict()` (rejects unknown), `.catchall(schema)` (validates unknown keys against a schema).

sections.title: Arrays, tuples, records, maps, and sets
sections.body: Arrays: `z.array(schema)` or `schema.array()`. Constrain with `.nonempty()`, `.min(n)`, `.max(n)`, `.length(n)`. Access `.element` for the inner schema. Tuples: `z.tuple([s1, s2, ...])` for fixed-length typed arrays; add `.rest(schema)` for variadic tail. Records: `z.record(keySchema, valueSchema)` for dictionaries. Maps: `z.map(keySchema, valueSchema)`. Sets: `z.set(schema)` with `.nonempty()`, `.min()`, `.max()`, `.size()` constraints.

sections.title: Enums, unions, and discriminated unions
sections.body: Enums: `z.enum(["a", "b"])` creates a string literal union with `.enum` access and `.options` array. `z.nativeEnum(TsEnum)` wraps TypeScript enums. Unions: `z.union([s1, s2])` or `s1.or(s2)` — Zod tries each schema in order. Discriminated unions: `z.discriminatedUnion("key", [schemas])` — uses a shared literal field to pick the right branch, giving much better error messages and performance than plain unions. Intersections: `z.intersection(s1, s2)` or `s1.and(s2)`.

sections.title: Recursive and lazy schemas
sections.body: Use `z.lazy(() => schema)` for recursive types like trees and linked lists. You must provide an explicit TypeScript type annotation: `const Schema: z.ZodType<MyType> = z.object({ children: z.lazy(() => Schema.array()) })`. Without the annotation, TypeScript cannot infer recursive types. This pattern works for self-referential structures, mutually recursive schemas, and JSON-like types.

scripts.name: Create Zod Schema
scripts.description: Generate a Zod schema from a TypeScript interface description or JSON example, with full type inference and validation

shape.description: Object describing the data shape — keys are field names, values are Zod type strings (e.g. 'string', 'number', 'string[]', 'enum:a,b,c')

strict.description: Whether to use .strict() mode to reject unknown keys

code.description: Generated TypeScript code defining the Zod schema

depends.reason: Zod must be installed in the project
